note: Code-level synthesis of round-4 decoder rule additions. Real after-round-4 export must be regenerated via the browser /verify flow because blind inference runs in-browser only (MediaPipe + WebGL). The 'before-round-4' artifacts are byte-identical to the real after-round-3 export and remain the verified ground truth for the code prior to round-4 rules.

newRerankRules.behavior: Undo over-eager chop -> big-fall promotion when release/aftermath are weak, repeated strokes are present, compact handshape changes are embedded, directional consistency is low, and the margin collapsed near zero. Demote big-fall to repeated-tool-use-like instead of leaving the chop/big-fall pair tied.
newRerankRules.expectedImpactOnSample2: seg-06 (currently big-fall-like vs chop/cut-like, margin 0, motif tags include repeated-action-loop) is the strongest candidate to flip to repeated-tool-use-like under this rule. Removes the only big-fall-like vs chop/cut-like confusion entry.

newRerankRules.behavior: Sharpening boost (no swap). When chop/cut-like already wins with narrow consistent vertical strokes, boost chop and reduce fingerspell so the margin widens past the low-confidence-competition threshold. Re-rank afterward.
newRerankRules.expectedImpactOnSample2: seg-02 / seg-03 (currently chop/cut-like, runner-up fingerspell, margins 0.097 / 0.0927, motif tags include repeated-action-loop and repeated-compact-handshape-sequence). Either the rule fires and widens margins past 0.097 + 0.06 = ~0.16 (clear of low-confidence-competition for the pair), or the directional/amplitude consistency is below 0.55 / 0.5 and the rule does not fire (no regression). Confusion-pair count for chop/cut-like vs fingerspell expected to drop from 2 toward 1 or 0.

newRerankRules.behavior: Sharpening boost (no swap). When big-fall-like already wins with strong release plus rebound aftermath and unstable end-state, boost big-fall and reduce fingerspell so the margin widens.
newRerankRules.expectedImpactOnSample2: seg-08 (big-fall-like vs fingerspell, margin 0.145). If rule fires, margin widens to ~0.21+; if release/aftermath thresholds fail, no flip and no regression. Mirrors round-3 rule C (which demotes big-fall when bursts dominate without release).

expectedConfusionPairDeltas.chop/cut-like vs fingerspell/emphatic-le: 2 -> 1 or 0 (rule B widens margin past competition threshold)
expectedConfusionPairDeltas.big-fall-like vs fingerspell/emphatic-le: 1 -> 0 or 1 (rule C widens margin if release/aftermath thresholds met)

expectedFailureTagDeltas.low-confidence-competition: 10 -> 7..9 (depends on how many margins widen past the tag threshold)
expectedFailureTagDeltas.tool-use-vs-release-confusion: 3 -> 2 (seg-06 may shed this tag after flip)
